acceptance_targets: [CH09_CRAFT_OPERATIONAL_DEFERRAL] A cold reader of the revised safe-house sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:111, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:167 can identify one explicit on-page reason no immediate report is made and can say which character makes or forces that choice. Before the kiss, the scene must show either an attempted call, a stated refusal with concrete consequence, or a clearly articulated compromise risk tied to WISE contact or the phone in the apartment.

[CH09_INTIMACY_FADEOUT] Pass if the revised closing of Chapter 9 (snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2437-2469) establishes physical proximity as an ongoing pressure through at least one concrete sensory detail that persists after the kiss ends, rather than immediately converting physical intimacy into abstract moral thesis. Fail if the kiss still functions as a sealed-off event with no physical continuity into the scene's closing beats.

[MORAL_META_INVENTORY_CH09] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2449-2465 no longer contains the Loid/Yor compartmentalization comparison or the multi-sentence philosophical diagnosis of telepathic emotional contamination, and the closing relies on the concrete physical details of the safe-house scene (sleep, name, bulb, blood) rather than moral inventory. Fail if the chapter still invokes family comparisons or explicitly theorizes about whether Anya's feelings are 'real' or 'contamination' in paragraph-length philosophical passages.
[RED-022] Ch 9 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.
[RED-033] Ch 9 'She'-opener sentences are at or below 7% of total sentences.
[RED-044] Ch 9 'something' count is at or below 1 per 800 prose words.
[LW04-001] A reader moving directly from Chapter 8 into Chapter 9 can explain why this specific safe location is still safe despite the WISE-mole reveal and the apparent schedule leak.
[LW04-003] By the end of Chapter 10, a reader can point to one concrete development in the mole plot beyond Anya privately worrying that it exists.

[LW05-001] After chapter 9, a reader should feel that Damian is close to placing her, but should not be able to point to an earlier internal full-name identification. Chapter 12 should be the first unambiguous moment where Damian fully locks Anya to Anya Forger.

findings.acceptance_test: A cold reader of the revised safe-house sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:111, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:167 can identify one explicit on-page reason no immediate report is made and can say which character makes or forces that choice. Before the kiss, the scene must show either an attempted call, a stated refusal with concrete consequence, or a clearly articulated compromise risk tied to WISE contact or the phone in the apartment.
findings.problem: After a coordinated assassination attempt tied to a restricted ministry schedule and a suspected leak inside WISE, the chapter admits that Anya should secure the perimeter, report upward, or begin immediate analysis, then simply postpones all of it so the intimacy beat can happen. Because the scene never shows a failed attempt, a concrete obstacle, or an explicit costly choice about why the prepaid phone and WISE emergency line cannot be used now, the professional logic reads as hand-waved instead of pressured.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the safe-house transition and aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:168, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:251, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:252 so the operational deferral is dramatized, not merely acknowledged. Use the phone introduced at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:69 as a live decision point: either Anya attempts contact and aborts for a specific reason, or she and Damian explicitly decide not to report because of a named compromise risk, trace risk, or exposure cost. Then make the late 'tomorrow' turn grow out of that on-page decision rather than arriving as a free-floating postponement.

findings.problem: The Chapter 9 kiss is the only physical intimacy between Anya and Damian in the entire novel. After lines 2405-2437 establish a kiss that escalates from tentative to urgent — with his hand on her hip, her hand on the back of his neck, his arm tightening — the narrative retreats entirely from the relationship's physical dimension. Eight subsequent chapters contain zero physical contact between them: no touch in the Chapter 13 basement meeting, no proximity in the Chapter 15 summit, no physical acknowledgment in the Chapter 17 dinner. Two twenty-year-olds who have acknowledged mutual attraction and shared an emotionally intense kiss do not then interact for months as pure diplomatic colleagues. The novel establishes physical desire and then sanitizes it out of existence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2437-2469 to plant the physical dimension as an ongoing pressure rather than a one-time event. After the kiss ends (line 2437), instead of immediately launching into moral inventory, let the physical proximity persist — their bodies still close, the awareness of contact continuing — so that the reader understands this is a threshold crossed, not a moment sealed off. The physical dimension should register as something that will continue to exert pressure in subsequent chapters, not as something the narrative has finished with. Strategy option: the closing paragraphs of Chapter 9 could include a concrete physical detail — his hand still on her back, her reluctance to create distance — that the reader carries forward as an expectation the later chapters must address.

acceptance_test.excerpt:
2449: She cared. She cared in a way he couldn't see and she couldn't explain and would never be able to justify. Because the feelings moving through her right now — the want, the tenderness, the specific ache of being close to someone who was close to her — she couldn't separate them from his. She'd been inside his mind for the most unguarded moment of his life, and her emotional responses were tangled with his emotional output, and she didn't know where his feelings ended and hers began.
2450: 
2451: That was the thing about telepathy that no one would ever understand, because no one knew. It didn't just give you information. It gave you feelings that weren't yours and made them indistinguishable from the ones that were. She had felt his loneliness, and now she felt lonely. She had felt his hope, and now she was hopeful. Was that connection? Or was it just contamination she'd mistaken for something real?

2453: She thought about Loid. About the way he could compartmentalize — separate what he felt from what he did, keep the operation clean of personal debris. She'd trained under him for years, and she'd never learned that trick. Maybe you couldn't learn it when the personal...

evidence.excerpt: 2449: She cared. She cared in a way he couldn't see and she couldn't explain and would never be able to justify. Because the feelings moving through her right now — the want, the tenderness, the specific ache of being close to someone who was close to her — she couldn't separate them from his. She'd been inside his mind for the most unguarded moment of his life, and her emotional responses were tangled with his emotional output, and she didn't know where his feelings ended and hers began.

2451: That was the thing about telepathy that no one would ever understand, because no one knew. It didn't just give you information. It gave you feelings that weren't yours and made them indistinguishable from the ones that were. She had felt his loneliness, and now she felt lonely. She had felt his hope, and now she was hopeful. Was that connection? Or was it just contamination she'd mistaken for something real?

2453: She thought about Loid. About the way he could compartmentalize — separate what he felt from what he did, keep the operation clean of personal debris. She'd trained under him for years, and she'd never learned that trick. Maybe you couldn't learn it when the personal debris arrived in other people's voices, uninvited, indistinguishable from your own thoughts.

2455: She thought about Yor. About the stillness her mother carried after an assignment, the careful way she washed her hands, the gap between the sweetness and the thing underneath. Yor managed by not looking. Anya couldn't not look. That was her whole problem.

2465: She had kissed him. She had read his mind while she kissed him. She had heard him think her real name and she had kept kissing him anyway. These were facts. She arranged them in order and they did not become less true.

findings.problem: Chapter 9's post-kiss closing (lines 2449-2469) restates the moral-inventory pattern after the novel's most emotionally charged scene. Line 2449-2451 converts the felt experience of the kiss into philosophical diagnosis of telepathic contamination ('It gave you feelings that weren't yours and made them indistinguishable from the ones that were'). Lines 2453-2455 invoke Loid's compartmentalization and Yor's stillness as comparison frameworks — the same family-comparison structure used in chapters 3 and 4. Line 2465 explicitly arranges the scene's events into a moral ledger: 'These were facts. She arranged them in order and they did not become less true.' The kiss scene has already dramatized everything these paragraphs state.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise lines 2449-2465 (FINAL_NOVEL). Remove the Loid/Yor compartmentalization comparison paragraphs and compress the philosophical diagnosis of telepathic emotional contamination to at most one sentence. The closing must rest on the concrete elements already present: Damian asleep, his thoughts thinning, her name surfacing and fading in his drifting mind, the bare bulb buzzing, the blood on her fingers, the bruise she hasn't looked at. If the contamination/borrowed-feelings question survives, it arrives through one concrete image, not abstract essay. Add at least one observable consequence after the kiss (a changed behavior, a physical detail, an operational implication) rather than another abstract summary of what telepathy means.

findings.acceptance_test: A reader moving directly from Chapter 8 into Chapter 9 can explain why this specific safe location is still safe despite the WISE-mole reveal and the apparent schedule leak.
findings.evidence: Chapter 8 ends with Anya deciding she must investigate a WISE mole in secret because normal channels are unsafe, then Chapter 9 links Damian's ambush to leaked access and immediately moves him into a WISE fallback apartment anyway. snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2200 snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2273 snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2283

evidence.excerpt: 2200: She would need to find the mole herself. Quietly. Without telling WISE what she was looking for or why she was looking. She would investigate her own agency using tradecraft her father had taught her and an ability she'd never disclosed to him, conducting an unauthorized investigation inside an organization she'd already lied to, on the basis of information she'd obtained through a method that no one in the world knew existed.

2273: She was already thinking about the precision of it. They'd known which meeting. They'd known what time he'd leave, which route he'd take. The meeting hadn't been publicly scheduled — it was a closed session with ministry staff. Someone with access to Damian's calendar, or access to the intelligence networks that tracked it.

2283: The WISE fallback apartment was in a residential block in the west district, far from both the diplomatic quarter and the government buildings. Anya had memorized its location during her first week in Berlint and had never used it. The building's front door opened with a six-digit code she had to enter twice because her hands were shaking. The apartment was on the third floor, no elevator, and by the time they reached it she was holding her left side with her arm and breathing through her teeth.

findings.problem: Read back-to-back, Anya behaves as if WISE is compromised enough to hide from but still the obvious place to shelter inside. Without one line establishing why this apartment sits outside the mole's access path, the move reads like the chapter forgetting the institutional breach it just made urgent.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either route them to a non-WISE refuge, or add concrete tactical reasoning that this fallback is off-book from analyst access, unused in system records, or controlled only by a compartment the mole cannot see.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of Chapter 10, a reader can point to one concrete development in the mole plot beyond Anya privately worrying that it exists.
findings.evidence: Chapter 8 frames the mole as an immediate private emergency and sends Anya into a self-directed hunt, but Chapter 9 jumps three days ahead, converts that urgency into generalized suspicion around Damian, and then postpones actual mole work to tomorrow just before Chapter 10 leaves the thread again. snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2200 snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2221 snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2467

evidence.excerpt: 2200: She would need to find the mole herself. Quietly. Without telling WISE what she was looking for or why she was looking. She would investigate her own agency using tradecraft her father had taught her and an ability she'd never disclosed to him, conducting an unauthorized investigation inside an organization she'd already lied to, on the basis of information she'd obtained through a method that no one in the world knew existed.

2221: Anya had been following Damian for twenty minutes, keeping a block and a half between them since he left the Foreign Affairs Ministry. A late meeting, the building mostly dark behind him. She'd told herself this was standard protocol — monitoring an operational partner in an unsecured environment. Which was partly true. The rest of the truth was that three days after catching a thought-fragment about *the asset inside WISE* at the diplomatic reception, she couldn't leave Damian's movements unchecked.

2467: Tomorrow she'd think about the ambush — about who had access to his schedule, about the chain of intelligence that had placed two military-trained killers on exactly the right block at exactly the right moment. Tomorrow she'd think about the mole inside WISE, and about the gaps in the operational picture that someone was exploiting, and about whether those gaps connected to a false report she'd filed in a debriefing room months ago while reading her handler's trust. Tomorrow she would be the operative again.

findings.problem: The window asks the reader to carry the WISE-mole reveal as a live wire, but the next two chapter boundaries keep deferring concrete movement on it. The ambush should sharpen that line; instead it becomes background anxiety while the window spends its capital elsewhere.
findings.rewrite_direction: Give Chapter 9 or early Chapter 10 one tangible mole-facing advance: a narrowed suspect pool, a specific causal link between the ambush and WISE access, or a small unauthorized investigative move that changes leverage before the window closes.

findings.acceptance_test: After chapter 9, a reader should feel that Damian is close to placing her, but should not be able to point to an earlier internal full-name identification. Chapter 12 should be the first unambiguous moment where Damian fully locks Anya to Anya Forger.

evidence.excerpt: 2361: "You remind me of someone," he said.

2365: "I know." *Anya Forger. Her name was Anya. She had pink hair and she punched me during dodgeball and her laugh was—* The thought was clear and specific and right there, a name on the tip of his mind, not yet spoken but forming. He rubbed his eyes with the back of his hand. "Sorry. Getting hit in the head makes me nostalgic, apparently."
2423: *Anya Forger.*

2457: He settled back against the wall, his shoulder touching hers. The apartment was cold and neither of them moved to find a blanket or turn on a heater that probably didn't work anyway. His thoughts were drifting toward sleep — the exhaustion of adrenaline and injury finally catching up — and as they drifted, the name surfaced again. *Anya Forger.* Quiet this time. Almost fond. Not a deduction anymore, just a feeling. Like a song you used to know the words to.

2465: She had kissed him. She had read his mind while she kissed him. She had heard him think her real name and she had kept kissing him anyway. These were facts. She arranged them in order and they did not become less true.
3146: *She's Anya Forger.*

1935: He thought about the cultural attache he'd met at a state dinner. The woman with the quick smile and the laugh that caught him somewhere he hadn't expected. The way she'd tilted her head when she was listening — not politely but with a kind of total attention that made him feel like the only frequency in a crowded room.

2091: *— she looks like someone. Not just familiar. She looks like — Eden Academy. The school festival. Second year. We got assigned to the same cleanup crew and she knocked over an entire bucket of soapy water on Henderson's shoes and when she laughed she —*

findings.problem: Chapter 9 already lets Damian reach Anya's exact name in thought, then repeat it in sleep, so when chapter 12 frames the gala beat as the decisive lock-clicking recognition, the cluster reads it as confirmation of a conclusion he has effectively already had rather than the first irreversible identification.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch 9, keep Damian at sensory and memory recognition WITHOUT the fully articulated name 'Anya Forger.' He can think of pink hair, dodgeball, the school festival, a specific girl, the punch — but the name must not resolve on the page. The closest he should come is a fragment that breaks off or is displaced by pain, adrenaline, or the kiss. Ch 12 is the intended detonation point where a drunken classmate says 'Anya Forger' and Damian's thoughts lock into place; Ch 13's Damian POV is where he privately confirms. Ch 9 must be the closest he's come without arriving. Also differentiate Ch 9's recognition mechanics from Ch 8's near-miss: change the stimulus (not another across-the-room trigger), add a behavioral consequence (spoken question, visible misstep, tactical adjustment), or let the intimacy produce a qualitatively different kind of memory access than the reception did.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
